## Highlights

First minor bump since the [v0.2.4 release](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/releases/tag/0.2.4)
in May 2025. Headline themes:

- **Dropped the `libvirt-python` C-extension dependency.** The CLI
    now shells out to `virsh`, `qemu-img`, and `virt-install` for all
    hypervisor operations. `uv sync` works without `libvirt-dev` or
    `pkg-config` on the host.
- **CLI ported from Click to Typer**, plus a parallel port of
    `createvm` and `destroyvm`.
- **`createvm` / `destroyvm` ship as standalone console scripts**
    alongside `lvlab` — one-off VM workflows without writing a
    manifest.
- **`qemu:///session` support via user-mode networking** — `lvlab`
    works rootless without managed libvirt networks.
- **New `lvlab ssh-config` command** emits `~/.ssh/config` snippets
    for managed VMs.
- **Cross-distro interface naming** — the v2 (netplan) network-config
    template now uses `match.driver: virtio_net` + `set-name`, so the
    manifest's `iface.name` is operator-chosen and works the same on
    every supported distro (no more "use enp1s0 on Debian / eth0 on
    AlmaLinux" footgun). Existing manifests with kernel-style names
    keep working.
- **End-to-end validated** on Debian 12, Debian 13, AlmaLinux 10,
    Fedora 44 via the new
    `scripts/host-bootstrap.sh` + `scripts/run-validation.sh`
    procedure.

## Breaking changes

- **Build-time `libvirt-dev` / `pkg-config` no longer required.**
    Not breaking in the "your install will fail" sense, but if your
    Dockerfile / Ansible role / VM image installs those packages
    specifically for `tkc-lvlab`, you can drop them. Runtime still
    needs `libvirt-clients` (or equivalent) for the `virsh` binary.
- **`lvlab status` no longer reports a libvirt domain "reason" string.**
    The Phase 2 port to `virsh` drops the C-API-only reason field —
    `status` now reports state only (`running`, `shut off`, etc.).
    If you parse `lvlab status` output programmatically, check it.

## Install / upgrade

```bash
uv tool install --upgrade \
  https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/releases/download/0.3.0/tkc_lvlab-0.3.0-py3-none-any.whl
```

Host package list per distro is in
[`scripts/host-bootstrap.sh`](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/blob/0.3.0/scripts/host-bootstrap.sh)
— that script is the source of truth for "what to apt/dnf install"
on each supported host.

## Supported hosts

Validated end-to-end on:

- **Debian 12** bookworm (Python 3.11)
- **Debian 13** trixie (Python 3.13)
- **AlmaLinux 10** (Python 3.12)
- **Fedora 44** (Python 3.14)

Out of scope: Debian 11, Ubuntu 22.04/24.04, AlmaLinux 9, older Fedora.
See [`docs-extra/host-validation.md`](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/blob/0.3.0/docs-extra/host-validation.md)
for the full matrix and the bootstrap/validation procedure.

## New robustness work

- **osinfo-db fallback chain** — `virt-install --os-variant`
    requests now survive on hosts whose `osinfo-db` predates the
    requested variant. A Debian 12 host (osinfo-db from 2022-11-30)
    can deploy `debian13` VMs; the fallback resolves to the highest
    known same-family variant and the deploy succeeds with a warning.
- **System-first PATH for `virt-install` subprocess** — fixes a
    Debian 13 portability bug where trixie's `virt-install` uses
    `#!/usr/bin/env python3` (every other distro hard-codes
    `/usr/bin/python3`), so without the override the venv's Python
    gets selected and `import gi` fails.
- **`lvlab up` exits non-zero when deploy fails** — previously
    logged an error but returned 0, breaking shell pipelines.
- **`/etc/hosts` cloud-init runcmd verifies existing entries** before
    appending, instead of unconditionally adding (and producing
    duplicates on re-up).
- **Integration test suite** with prefix-scoped resource ownership
    and AST-based static safety lint to prevent runaway test cleanup
    from touching developer VMs.

## Toolchain

- Migrated from Poetry to **uv** (`uv_build` PEP 517 backend).
- **src-layout** — package source moved to `src/tkc_lvlab/`.
- **Zensical** replaces MkDocs + Material as the doc engine.
- API reference auto-generated from Google-style docstrings + type
    hints via `mkdocstrings`.
